meta.title: Kurjun — Archive of Stories
meta.desc: Kurjun — a traditional bag as a metaphor for storing and passing on stories, knowledge and memory.

hero.caption1: Each piece holds a story. Each story continues a life.
hero.caption2: Keeping culture alive through women's hands

intro.quote: This is a collection of stories of women craftswomen from the Naryn region. Through their work, they create, teach, and shape everyday life around them.
intro.sub: This project explores how women use traditional craft in their everyday lives — to create, work, and shape their communities.

context.p1: The women shared different stories — each shaped by their own experiences, choices, and paths.
context.p2: They use craft in their own ways: to work, to support themselves, to teach others, and to build connections with people around them.
context.p3: Through what they do, they shape their lives, their communities, and what these practices mean today.

s1.title: Leather remembers hands
s1.text: Every scratch is not a defect. It is the trace of a touch, a road, a passing from one pair of hands to another.

s2.title: Felt and warmth
s2.text: Felt is not woven — it is pressed. Fibres entangle slowly, under pressure and heat. So do memories.

s3.title: Passing on
s3.text: The Kurjun passes from mother to daughter, father to son. Inside it — not only objects. Inside it — a way of life, a silent conversation across time.

s4.title: The weight of things
s4.text: Not everything in the bag is heavy. Some objects weigh almost nothing, yet you would never leave them behind.

s5.title: The thread and the pattern
s5.text: A single thread means nothing. Together they form something that cannot be reduced to its parts — a pattern, a story, a life.

about.heading: About the project
about.body: "Kurjun is a digital space built around the stories of women who hold the threads of our identity. It is not merely an archive of objects, but a ledger of existence carved from the memory of the Tien Shan mountains."
about.col1: This is an archive of women from the Naryn region — an archive of their craft, their experience, and their lives. Not only an archive of objects, but of the people behind them and their words.
about.col2: Each story lives on this site — as memory, text, and image. This site is a directory to the world of these women: an opportunity to find them, listen to them, and learn about them.

credits.heading: Authors & Acknowledgements
credits.desc: Kurjun is a project collecting stories of women craftswomen from the Naryn region. The project was made with their permission and support.
